source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Accidental Discharge of Zinc Bromide Brine
actions_taken: Wellhead casing valve was closed to stop the discharge
NRC Report #1368219 submitted on May 25, 2023 at 11:08am
BSEE Lake Charles District notified via email on May 25, 2023 at 12:39pm
event.summary: On May 25, 2023 at 01:15 hours, approximately 79 barrels of zinc bromide brine was inadvertently discharged from a wellhead casing valve at Well Number C007 on the Enterprise 264 rig during BOP testing and production casing refill operations. The Floorhand failed to fully close the 1 13/16-inch 10M gate valve casing valve, believing it to be closed when it was not. The valve outlet was positioned beneath the deck and not visible during pumping operations, contributing to the failure to detect the incomplete closure.

hazards.name: Pressurized zinc bromide brine in wellhead system
Wellhead casing valve in open or partially open position

threats.name: Incomplete closure of wellhead casing valve during pumping operations
Failure to verify valve closure before pressurization
Inadequate visibility of valve outlet position during operations

consequences.name: Discharge of zinc bromide brine to Gulf of Mexico environment

controls.name: Wellhead casing valve closure verification
controls.barrier_role: Prevent pressurized fluid discharge by ensuring valve is fully closed
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent loss of containment

supporting_text: The Floorhand closed the casing valve on the wellhead to what 'he believed to be the closed position'
The wellhead casing valve was not fully closed during the zinc bromide pumping operation, which allowed approximately 79 barrels to flow
The wellhead casing valve was previously opened by a different member of the drill crew other than the Floorhand attempting to close it

controls.name: Pit Volume Totalizer alarm system monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Detect abnormal fluid loss and alert driller to potential discharge
controls.lod_basis: Secondary detection system to identify loss of circulation

supporting_text: The Pit Volume Totalizer is used to describe a series of devices on the Enterprise 264 that continuously monitor the level of fluids held in the pits
If the level drops too low or rises too high, the alarm sounds to warn the driller of lost circulation or an influx of wellbore fluids
The Floorhand 'noticed it was taking longer than normal to fill and get returns'
The Driller 'looked in the hole and returns were coming over the bell nipple but slow'

controls.name: Visual inspection and monitoring of wellhead casing valve during operations
controls.barrier_role: Verify valve position and detect incomplete closure before pressurization
controls.lod_basis: Direct observation to confirm valve closure status

supporting_text: The position of the 1 13/16-inch outlet was beneath the deck and not visible to the Floorhand while attempting to close the casing valve
The wellhead casing valve was not visible at the time of pumping operations. Not monitored.
Because of this, the Floorhand was sent by the Driller to recheck the valve for full closure

controls.name: Procedure for valve closure and verification by same operator
controls.barrier_role: Ensure continuity of knowledge and responsibility for valve status
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to prevent knowledge gaps between operators

supporting_text: The wellhead casing valve was previously opened by a different member of the drill crew other than the Floorhand attempting to close it during the operation
A member of the drill crew initially opened the casing valve. Another member of the drill crew, a Floorhand, was assigned to monitor the valve for flow

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
